setup.title: BEEP Kenshi Mod Manager
setup.description: Works with Steam and GOG installations. Click Auto-Detect or browse to your Kenshi game folder manually.
setup.gamePathHelp: The folder containing kenshi_x64.exe or kenshi.exe — NOT the Mods subfolder
setup.steamPathHelp: GOG users: leave this blank. Your mods in the Kenshi/Mods folder are detected automatically.
detail.selectMod: Select a mod to view details
detail.author: Author:
packs.title: Mod Packs
packs.loadConfirm: Load mod pack "{name}"? This will replace your current categories and load order.
conflicts.title: Conflicts
conflicts.selectMod: Select a mod to view its conflicts, or click "Check Conflicts" in the toolbar first.
conflicts.clickCheck: Click "Check Conflicts" in the toolbar to scan your mods.
conflicts.noConflicts: No conflicts detected for this mod.
zoneMap.title: Zone Conflict Map
tutorial.heading: Beep!
tutorial.welcome: Welcome to BEEP Kenshi Mod Manager
tutorial.organize: Organize mods — drag and drop mods between category columns to sort your load order
tutorial.conflicts: Deep conflict detection — detects data, asset, and level conflicts with actual values shown inline
tutorial.conflictMap: Conflict map — interactive Kenshi world map showing exterior and interior conflict zones
tutorial.aiSort: AI sorting — export your mod list, let AI suggest an order, then import it
tutorial.pinUncat: Pin Uncategorized — pin uncategorized mods for easy drag-and-drop sorting
tutorial.themes: Kenshi theme — toggle between Dark and Kenshi themes in the toolbar
tutorial.packs: Mod Packs — save and load complete mod configurations
tutorial.gettingStartedText: BEEP helps you organize your Kenshi mods into categories displayed as columns. Load order reads left-to-right, top-to-bottom. Your existing mods.cfg load order is preserved on first launch.
update.title: Update Available!
aiGuide.title: AI Sort Guide
aiGuide.responseFormat: What the AI response should look like
aiExport.title: AI Sort Export
aiExport.warning: Note: You have a large mod list. "Compact" mode is recommended — it fits everything in one prompt. "Full Descriptions" may exceed AI token limits.
aiImport.title: Paste AI Response
aiImport.placeholder: Paste the AI's sorted load order here...
status.importedGeneric: Imported generic mod list ({count} mods reordered in Uncategorized).
status.updateAvailable: Update v{version} available! Download manually from GitHub.
status.allSorted: No uncategorized mods to sort — everything is already placed!
status.aiApplied: AI order applied: {matched} mods sorted successfully!
status.aiAppliedPartial: AI order applied: {matched} mods sorted, {unmatched} could not be matched.
status.depWarning: Warning: {count} dependency issues across {mods} mods — click mods highlighted in red for details.
detect.notFound: Could not find Kenshi. Use Browse to set paths manually.
detect.failed: Detection failed. Use Browse to set paths manually.
confirm.deleteCategory:
Delete category "{name}"?

All mods will be moved to Uncategorized.
confirm.resetCategories: Reset all categories? All mods will move to Uncategorized and default categories will be restored.
confirm.loadPack: Load mod pack "{name}"? This will replace your current categories and load order.
confirm.updateAvailable:
Update available: v{current} → v{latest}

Download and install? The app will restart automatically.
tutorial.overview.organize: Organize mods — drag and drop mods between category columns to sort your load order
tutorial.overview.conflicts: Deep conflict detection — detects data, asset, and level conflicts with actual values shown inline
tutorial.overview.conflictMap: Conflict map — interactive Kenshi world map showing exterior and interior conflict zones
tutorial.overview.aiSort: AI sorting — export your mod list, let AI suggest an order, then import it
tutorial.overview.pinUncat: Pin Uncategorized — pin uncategorized mods for easy drag-and-drop sorting
tutorial.overview.themes: Kenshi theme — toggle between Dark and Kenshi themes in the toolbar
tutorial.overview.packs: Mod Packs — save and load complete mod configurations
tutorial.sorting.1: Drag and drop mods between category columns to sort your load order.
tutorial.sorting.2: Multi-select with Ctrl+click (toggle) or Shift+click (range), then right-click to move them all to a category at once.
tutorial.sorting.3: Group drag — select multiple mods and drag them together.
tutorial.sorting.4: Pin Uncategorized to get a resizable side panel for easy sorting.
tutorial.sorting.5: Drag column headers to reorder entire categories.
tutorial.categories.1: Add categories with the "+ Add Category" button in the toolbar.
tutorial.categories.2: Right-click a category header to rename, change color, or delete it.
tutorial.categories.3: Drag category headers left/right to reorder them. Load order follows category order.
tutorial.categories.4: Reset Categories restores the default category set.
tutorial.packs.1: Go to the Packs tab to save and load complete mod configurations.
tutorial.packs.2: Save Current captures your entire setup — categories, mod order, and active/inactive state.
tutorial.packs.3: Load a pack to instantly restore a previous configuration.
tutorial.packs.4: Great for switching between different playthroughs or saving before AI sort.
tutorial.importExport.1: Export List — saves your full setup (categories, load order, active/inactive state) to a text file. Share it with friends or back it up.
tutorial.importExport.2: Import List — loads a previously exported BEEP mod list, restoring all categories and ordering.
tutorial.importExport.3: Generic import — also supports plain lists of .mod filenames (one per line). These reorder your uncategorized mods to match the list.
tutorial.conflicts.1: Check Conflicts deep-parses .mod files, level data, and asset files to find real conflicts.
tutorial.conflicts.2: Green = this mod wins, Red = overridden, Yellow = both overridden by a third mod.
tutorial.conflicts.3: Expand any property to see the actual values each mod sets. Identical values are filtered out.
tutorial.conflicts.4: Click Map to see conflicts on the Kenshi world map with zoom, pan, and exterior/interior toggle.
tutorial.other.1: Themes — toggle Dark/Kenshi theme in the toolbar.
tutorial.other.2: Workshop tab — browse Steam Workshop directly in the app.
tutorial.other.3: Modding Guide tab — Kenshi load order guide from Steam.
tutorial.other.4: Auto-update — checks for new versions on launch.
tutorial.aiSort.desc: AI Sort uses an AI chatbot (Claude or ChatGPT) to automatically sort your mods into the correct Kenshi load order.
tutorial.aiSort.1: Save a mod pack first in the Packs tab so you can revert.
tutorial.aiSort.2: Click AI Sort Export — fetches Steam descriptions and builds a text file.
tutorial.aiSort.3: Paste the file into an AI chatbot and send it.
tutorial.aiSort.5: Click AI Sort Import, paste, and click Apply Order.
tutorial.aiSort.privacy: No data is sent automatically — you are the middleman. The app only contacts Steam's public API for descriptions.
tutorial.aiExport.1: Uncategorized Only — unsorted mods with full descriptions (recommended)
tutorial.aiExport.3: All Mods Compact — everything with short descriptions (500+ mods)
tutorial.aiExport.4: All Mods Full — complete descriptions (most accurate, largest)
tutorial.aiExport.5: Split Batches — multiple files with ~50 mods each
tutorial.aiExport.formatNote: Extra commentary is OK — the parser strips everything that isn't a [Category] header or a .mod filename.
aiExport.uncatDesc: Only fetches descriptions for mods in Uncategorized. Already-sorted mods are listed by filename only. Much smaller file, faster AI processing, lower cost.
aiExport.uncatCompactDesc: Only unsorted mods, using tags + truncated descriptions. Already-sorted mods listed by filename. Best balance of accuracy and size.
aiExport.compactDesc: All mods using Steam tags + first 200 characters. Fits 500+ mods in a single prompt. Use for a full re-sort without exceeding token limits.
aiExport.allDesc: Fetches full descriptions for every active mod. Most accurate but produces a very large file. May exceed AI token limits for 100+ mods.
aiExport.splitDesc: Exports multiple files with ~50 mods each (full descriptions). Run each through the AI separately. Most accurate but most tedious.
